---
title: "BTC DPT Visium HD Cohort - myCAF Differential Expression Analysis"
author: "Meredith Wetzel"
date: "2025-07-02"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  fig.path = "8_figures/",
  dev = "pdf")
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(fgsea)
library(DESeq2)
library(DT)
library(ggbiplot)
library(reshape2)
library(ggpubr)
library(CoGAPS)
library(SeuratDisk)
source(file = "../../../stacked_props_function.R")
options(future.globals.maxSize = 8000 * 1024^2)
set.seed(1234)
```

```{r include=FALSE}
obj.dir <- "/projects/btcpilot_visiumhd/objects/"
```

```{r}
# load in data
load(file = paste0(obj.dir,"final_codeCheck/CAFsubtypes_016um_seurObj.rda"))
```

```{r}
type.color <- c("maroon1", "orange", "darkturquoise")
names(type.color) <- c("myCAF", "CAF", "iCAF")
```

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=15}
SpatialDimPlot(sobj.caf, group.by = "Type_CAF", ncol = 3, pt.size.factor = 8, image.alpha = 0.5, cols = type.color)
```

```{r fig.height=6, fig.width=6}
props <- stack_cell_porportions(sobj.caf, group.by.x = "sample", group.by.stack = "Type_CAF")
plot_stack(props, group.by.x = "sample", group.by.stack = "Type_CAF") + scale_fill_manual(values = type.color)
```

# Pseudobulk DE - myCAF first

```{r warning=FALSE}
sample_list <- SplitObject(sobj.caf, split.by = "sample")
HC1.sobj <- sample_list[["HC1"]]
HC3.sobj <- sample_list[["HC3"]]
HC4.sobj <- sample_list[["HC4"]]
HC5.sobj <- sample_list[["HC5"]]
HC7.sobj <- sample_list[["HC7"]]
HC8.sobj <- sample_list[["HC8"]]
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# Extract raw counts for each
counts_1 = HC1.sobj[,HC1.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
counts_3 = HC3.sobj[,HC3.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
counts_4 = HC4.sobj[,HC4.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
counts_5 = HC5.sobj[,HC5.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
counts_7 = HC7.sobj[,HC7.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
counts_8 = HC8.sobj[,HC8.sobj$Type_CAF == "myCAF"]@assays$Spatial.016um$counts
```

```{r}
# HC1,HC2,HC3,HC4 = NR; HC5,HC7,HC8 = R # NOTE: no HC6 and HC2 in this object
groups1 = c('HC1','HC3','HC4')
groups2 = c('HC5','HC7','HC8')

# Non Responders
agg1 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_1))
colnames(agg1) <- "HC1_NR"
agg3 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_3))
colnames(agg3) <- "HC3_NR"
agg4 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_4))
colnames(agg4) <- "HC4_NR"

# Responders
agg5 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_5))
colnames(agg5) <- "HC5_R"
agg7 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_7))
colnames(agg7) <- "HC7_R"
agg8 = as.matrix(rowSums(counts_8))
colnames(agg8) <- "HC8_R"

agg_all = cbind(agg1, agg3, agg4, agg5, agg7, agg8)
```

```{r}
# create metadata for deseq2
meta = data.frame('annot' = colnames(agg_all), 'sample_id' = c('HC1','HC3','HC4','HC5','HC7','HC8'), 'responseType' = c('NR','NR','NR','R','R','R'))
rownames(meta) = colnames(agg_all)
meta
```

```{r}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(agg_all, 
                              colData = meta, 
                              design = ~ responseType)
```

```{r}
exprsDat = as.matrix(dds@assays@data$counts)
boxplot(log2(exprsDat+1), ylab='log2(counts+1)', xlab='samples', outline = F, las=2, cex.axis = 0.55, border = ifelse(apply(log2(exprsDat+1),2,median)==0,'red','black'), main = "Distribution of log-transformed counts myCAF") # remove sample 5
```

```{r}
dds <- dds[,-c(4)] # these don't follow cluster names, follow cluster order (remove HC5)
```

```{r}
exprsDat = as.matrix(dds@assays@data$counts)
boxplot(log2(exprsDat+1), ylab='log2(counts+1)', xlab='samples', outline = F, las=2, cex.axis = 0.55, border = ifelse(apply(log2(exprsDat+1),2,median)==0,'red','black'), main = "Distribution of log-transformed counts myCAF") # check sample removal
```

```{r}
dds <- DESeq(dds, fitType = 'local') 
```

```{r}
# Plot dispersion estimates
plotDispEsts(dds, cex = 0.1)
```

```{r}
# Make comparisons of interest
        ## recommend to run all groups together, then use 'contrast' to extract comparisons of interest
#name_tocompare = 'NR_vs_R'
name_tocompare = "responseType_R_vs_NR" # makes no difference
cond = 'responseType'
v1 = 'NR'
v2 = 'R'
#The contrast = c(cond, v1, v2) is what's actually being used to calculate the results, and the name is essentially ignored
res <- results(dds, name = name_tocompare, contrast = c(cond, v1, v2), alpha = 0.05) #contrast = c('factorname','variable1tocompare','v2tocompare'))
        ## alpha is FDR-adjusted p value cutoff

summary(res)
```

```{r}
# Shrinking log fold changes for genes with low counts
    ## useful for visualization and gene ranking
    ## recommended for bulk RNAseq

levels(dds$responseType) # see what coef you want 

resLFC <- lfcShrink(dds, type = 'ashr', res = res, coef= 1) #, coef = 2 # there is no difference using coef = 1 or coef = 2
        ## coef is what level of factor you want to compare to reference level
        ## if res not provided, it will be generated using coef or contrast
resLFC
```

```{r}
# Remove NAs from p values
resLFC = resLFC[!is.na(resLFC$padj),]

# How many significant results?
length(resLFC@listData$padj)
length(resLFC@listData$padj[resLFC@listData$padj < 0.05])

# Extract stats for pathway analysis

resLFC_stats <- resLFC$log2FoldChange
names(resLFC_stats) = row.names(resLFC)
```

```{r}
plotMA(resLFC)
```

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=8}
EnhancedVolcano(resLFC, lab=row.names(resLFC), x='log2FoldChange', y='padj', FCcutoff = 0.5,pCutoff = 0.05, title = "NR vs. R in myCAF",labSize = 3)
```

```{r}
lfc_thresh <- 0.5
resLFC_TopDE <- resLFC[resLFC$padj < 0.05 & abs(resLFC$log2FoldChange) > lfc_thresh,]
resLFC_TopDE$abs_logFC = abs(resLFC_TopDE$log2FoldChange)

resLFC_TopDE = resLFC_TopDE[order(resLFC_TopDE$abs_logFC, decreasing = T),] #order by log2fc
nrow(resLFC_TopDE)
head(resLFC_TopDE, 10)
```

# Pathway analysis - HALLMARK

```{r}
HALLMARK <- gmtPathways("../../../h.all.v2023.2.Hs.symbols.gmt")
stats <- res$log2FoldChange
names(stats) <- rownames(res)
# trying to fix finite value error
stats <- stats[is.finite(stats)]
path_results <- list(HALLMARK=fgsea(pathways = HALLMARK, stats = stats))#, nPermSimple = 150000))
# Warning: There are ties in the preranked stats (7.01% of the list).
# The order of those tied genes will be arbitrary, which may produce unexpected results.Warning: There were 26 pathways for which P-values were not calculated properly due to unbalanced (positive and negative) gene-level statistic values. For such pathways pval, padj, NES, log2err are set to NA. You can try to increase the value of the argument nPermSimple (for example set it nPermSimple = 100000)
save(path_results, file = paste0(obj.dir,"final_codeCheck/FinalFigs_myCAF_pathwayRes_20251217.rda"))
```

```{r}
#path_results$HALLMARK <- path_results$HALLMARK[!is.na(path_results$HALLMARK$pval),]
path_results$HALLMARK$adjPvalue <- ifelse(path_results$HALLMARK$padj <= 0.05, "significant", "non-significant")
cols <- c("non-significant" = "grey", "significant" = "red")
ggplot(path_results$HALLMARK, aes(reorder(pathway, NES), NES, fill = adjPvalue)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=5)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x="Pathway", y="Normalized Enrichment Score",
       title="HALLMARK pathways Enrichment Score Non-Responder vs Responder in myCAF") + theme(plot.title.position = "plot", plot.title = element_text(size=8, face="bold"))
## Sig Only HALLMARK Barplot
path_results_sig <- path_results$HALLMARK[padj < 0.05]
if (nrow(path_results_sig)==0){ print("No Significant pathways with pAdj < 0.05"
)} else {
  ggplot(path_results_sig, aes(reorder(pathway, NES), NES, fill = adjPvalue)) +
    geom_col() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=5)) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x="Pathway", y="Normalized Enrichment Score",
         title="Significant (pAdj<0.05) HALLMARK pathways Enrichment Score Non-Responder vs Responder in myCAF") + theme(plot.title.position = "plot", plot.title = element_text(size=8, face="bold"))
}
```

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
